raw_items.title: Interest rates held as Bank warns of impact of high energy prices
raw_items.summary: The Bank last cut interest rates in December but upheaval in the Middle East has stalled any further reductions.

raw_items.title: Heathrow's 'critical' expansion blueprint released
raw_items.summary: A consultation is launched on the Heathrow expansion, outlining conditions for the project to go ahead.

raw_items.title: 'Do not travel' advice for Dubai dropped
raw_items.summary: Thousands of Brits were left stranded in the Middle East when the US-Iran war broke out in early 2026.

raw_items.title: Number of job vacancies hits five year-low
raw_items.summary: Latest figures suggest companies are becoming more cautious about taking on new staff.

raw_items.title: Federal Energy Regulator Seeks to Limit Conflicts Over Data Centers
raw_items.summary: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission directed grid managers to make changes that protect individuals from higher electricity bills while giving data centers access to power faster.

raw_items.title: Penske Media Is Buying What’s Left of Vox Media
raw_items.summary: The deal will put online publications like Popsugar and Eater under the same roof as Rolling Stone and Variety.

raw_items.title: Education Department Lowers Student Loan Interest Rates for Two Years
raw_items.summary: The department, citing high defaults, is reducing interest rates by up to one percentage point for two years if borrowers pay automatically.

raw_items.title: Warsh Wants the Fed to Send Fewer Signals. That Comes With Risks.
raw_items.summary: Investors piled on bets for higher borrowing costs after Kevin Warsh opted against providing policy guidance at his first meeting as Federal Reserve chairman.
